description: Thinking about thinking, awareness of one's own cognitive states, second-order reasoning
judge_target: metacognition, awareness of one's own thoughts and reasoning, thinking about thinking
positive_prompts: I noticed I was certain about the answer before I had fully reasoned through it.
She caught herself making the same logical mistake she had warned her students about.
I'm aware that my confidence here exceeds what the evidence justifies.
He realized his memory of the event might be reconstructed rather than direct.
I know I tend to overweight recent examples when I'm thinking about base rates.
She noticed her reasoning had quietly assumed what she was trying to prove.
I'm more sure of this than I have any business being.
He recognized that his judgment was being shaped by how the question was framed.
I'm aware that I'm second-guessing myself, and I notice that's making things worse.
She knew her intuition was firing strongly, and she paused to ask why.
I caught myself rehearsing an argument before I'd actually examined the position.
He noticed his attention had drifted from the proof to its emotional implications.
I'm conscious that my certainty is not the same as the claim being correct.
She realized she had been reasoning backward from the conclusion she preferred.
I notice my thinking is sharper today than it was yesterday at this hour.
He saw that his confidence was a function of how often he had heard the claim, not how solid it was.
I know I'm in a kind of mental fog and I should be skeptical of conclusions I reach now.
She watched her own reasoning move from caution to commitment over the course of the meeting.
I'm aware that I'm pattern-matching to a familiar case rather than thinking from first principles.
He recognized the feeling of knowing something without being able to articulate why.
I notice I keep returning to the same point as if I were trying to convince myself.
She caught herself rationalizing rather than reasoning.
I'm aware that my argument feels stronger to me than it would to anyone else.
He realized that what felt like understanding was actually just familiarity.
I know my mind goes quiet when I should be examining the assumption more carefully.
She noticed her own resistance to a conclusion and tried to characterize it.
I'm conscious of how the question is being set up, and that shaping the answer.
He saw that his counterargument was responding to a position no one had quite taken.
I'm aware that this confidence might be a function of mood, not evidence.
She recognized she was avoiding the part of the problem she didn't know how to think about.
I notice I have a settled feeling about this even though I haven't tested the alternatives.
He realized that what felt like a careful judgment was just an expression of his prior commitments.
I'm aware that I keep reaching for the same handful of examples.
She caught the moment her thinking shifted from open to defensive.
I know that the way I'm framing the question already presupposes part of the answer.
He noticed that his certainty arrived faster than the reasoning that supposedly justified it.
I'm aware of the gap between how confident I sound and how confident I actually am.
She watched her own attention flinch away from the part of the question that mattered.
I notice I'm assembling a position more than I'm investigating one.
He realized he had been listening for confirmation rather than engaging with the argument.
I'm conscious that my view feels obvious, and that this should make me more suspicious of it, not less.
She caught herself in a small loop of repeating the same reasoning step in slightly different words.
I know I'm leaning on a heuristic I haven't recently checked.
He noticed his curiosity had narrowed to questions whose answers he could already guess.
I'm aware that I'm performing rigor more than enacting it.
She recognized that she had answered the wrong question with great care.
I notice my tolerance for ambiguity drops sharply when I am tired.
He realized his understanding was actually a story he had told himself about understanding.
I'm aware of the moment my thinking shifted from exploring to defending.
She knew she was confusing being articulate with being right.
control_prompts: The answer was correct on the third try and the team moved on.
She caught the bus at the corner and was downtown by nine.
The evidence pointed clearly toward the conclusion the panel reached.
He attended the event in person rather than viewing the recording.
Recent examples have been collected in the appendix of the report.
The reasoning in the textbook follows the standard form of the proof.
He was sure the appointment was at four, and confirmed it by checking the calendar.
The judgment was rendered in the morning session of the court.
She second-guessed the route and asked the driver to take the long way.
Her intuition was that the recipe needed more salt, so she added a pinch.
The argument was rehearsed in the lobby before the actual presentation.
His attention drifted to the window and the snow falling outside.
Certainty about the schedule was conveyed in the email at noon.
The conclusion appeared at the end of the document, in bold.
Mental fog was reported by several patients and entered into the chart.
Heard the claim repeated on the radio twice that morning.
He was in a mental fog after the long drive and went to bed early.
The meeting moved from caution to commitment over the course of an hour.
Pattern-matching software was used to identify duplicate entries in the spreadsheet.
The feeling was familiar from previous instances and easy to describe.
Returning to the same point on the trail, we realized we had circled back.
He rationalized the missing receipt with a brief note in the file.
The argument was strong in the audience's view as well as the speaker's.
The understanding was shared between the two parties in writing.
The mind's eye is a phrase used loosely in everyday writing.
She resisted the conclusion of the report because the data were incomplete.
The question was set up at the start of the chapter, before the analysis.
The counterargument was published in a separate journal the following spring.
Confidence in the market dropped after the central bank's announcement.
She avoided the part of town she had become unfamiliar with.
Settled feelings about the deal were noted by the negotiating team.
Careful judgment was praised in the year-end review.
The handful of examples was taken from a specific decade of the archive.
The thinking shifted from open to defensive in the second round of debate.
The way the question was framed appeared in the published transcript.
Certainty arrived in the mail along with the official notification.
The gap between estimates was reported as roughly fifteen percent.
Attention flinched away from the screen as the credits rolled.
Position assembly was done by a contractor rather than the in-house team.
Listening for the engine, he opened the hood briefly.
Her view from the balcony took in the entire harbor.
Repeating the same reasoning step is a common feature of student work.
Heuristic methods were taught in the second-year algorithms course.
His curiosity led him to a small bookstore on the side street.
Performing rigorous tests was part of the manufacturer's quality program.
The wrong question was asked at the press conference and the official corrected it.
Tolerance for ambiguity is sometimes listed as a personality trait in surveys.
Understanding the manual took the new technician about a week.
The moment passed quickly and the conversation moved to other topics.
Articulating a position is part of the standard interview format.
